  • Changelog

    Module: cop_stochastic

    module-cop-stochastic.Rmd

    Purpose

    cop_stochastic performs stochastic correlates-of-protection analysis. It estimates vaccine efficacy as a function of a continuous immune marker (the “stochastic” CoP) using the causal inference framework of Gilbert, Fong, and Luedtke (2022). The analysis produces risk curves and VE surfaces as functions of hypothetical population-level marker distributions.

    Source module: correlates_reporting2/cop_stochastic/
    Key report: cop_stochastic/report.Rmd

    Example data 

    report_root  <- "/path/to/correlates_reporting2"
moderna_path <- file.path(
  report_root, "data_clean",
  "moderna_mock_data_processed_with_riskscore.csv"
)

    Reproduce the report

    1. Configure the session 

    library(utilrep2)

setup_session(
  trial       = "moderna_mock",
  platform    = "linux",
  data_path   = file.path(report_root, "data_clean"),
  report_root = report_root
)

    2. Run the module 

    run_cop_stochastic(trial = "moderna_mock")

    The module executes, in order:

    1. make_analysis_data.R — constructs the analysis dataset and writes it to data_clean/.
    2. run_cop_analysis.R — runs the stochastic CoP estimation.
    3. make_sve_cop_figs.R — generates VE surface and risk curve figures.

    With report = TRUE (the default), renders report.Rmd to PDF.

    run_cop_stochastic(trial = "moderna_mock", clean = FALSE, report = FALSE)

    Output

    Results are saved under cop_stochastic/output/<trial>/, including:

    • data_clean/*.rds — analysis-ready datasets
    • output/*.rds — model fit objects
    • figs/*.pdf — VE surface and risk curve figures
    • report.pdf — assembled report

    Report sections

    • Stochastic VE estimates as a function of marker level
    • Risk curves under shifted marker distributions
    • Sensitivity analyses

    Supported trials

    The module supports all trials that have a completed correlates-of-risk analysis. The clean argument removes data_clean/*.rds, output/*.rds, and figs/*.pdf from previous runs.

    Developed by Y H.

    Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
